hypothesis: If an EfficientNet-B0 model pretrained on the data-rich additive-free NCM SEM images is fine-tuned on the smaller additive-induced SEM dataset, then it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 for the nine NCM/aging classes than models trained only on the target data, and fine-tuning with targeted oversampling will yield better performance than using a weighted cross-entropy loss without oversampling because transfer learning leverages shared morphological features and oversampling mitigates class imbalance.
